label: section5_without_skill

messages_in.content: I'm reviewing a bracket design for a project similar to your CardioSense work. The bracket needs to handle a 500 lbf load. What did Ridgeline use for the CardioSense enclosure, and what's that load in newtons?

args.question: What material and thickness did Ridgeline use for the CardioSense enclosure bracket?

chunks.text:
Ridgeline Engineering Partners is a mechanical engineering consultancy headquartered in Portland, Oregon. The firm was founded in 2007 by Dr. Catherine Marsh and David Okonkwo, both former senior engineers at Bechtel, who saw an opportunity to provide specialized structural and thermal analysis services to mid-market clients who could not justify full-time engineering staff for niche technical problems.

Over the past eighteen years, the firm has grown from a two-person partnership to a consulta
chunks.title: Company Overview

chunks.text:
RIDGELINE ENGINEERING PARTNERS вЂ” TECHNICAL STANDARDS GUIDE
Revision 12, Updated September 2024
Maintained by: Lisa Fernandez, PE, Director of Quality

This document lists the primary technical standards referenced across Ridgeline's practice areas. Engineers must use the editions listed below unless a client contract specifies otherwise. Copies of all listed standards are available in the firm's digital standards library (SharePoint > Technical Resources > Standards Library).

STRUCTURAL ANALYSI
chunks.title: Technical Standards Reference Guide

result.summary: 2 chunks retrieved. Top match: DOC-001 вЂ” Company Overview (score 0.4744).

final_message:
Ridgeline's CardioSense enclosure details:
- I could not retrieve the specific material and thickness used for the CardioSense bracket in the information available. If you want a detailed spec, please let me know so I can refine the search.

As for your load:
- 500 lbf is equivalent to 2,224 N.

If you need recommendations for bracket material/thickness based on similar Ridgeline projects, I can dig deeper.
